summary: Ukrainian FPV drone operators are intensifying ambushes and strikes on Russian forces, including targeting air defense systems like the BUK near Bolshoy Sokolny Island, while also attempting to disrupt Russian troops before direct attacks. Russian forces are adapting by using log-periodic directional antennas on their "Molniyas" to increase range and resist jamming, though Ukrainian operators are exploiting polarization differences. Meanwhile, Russian anti-aircraft units are combining electronic warfare with small arms, particularly automatic rifles, to counter FPV kamikaze drones. On the innovation front, NATO has launched test ranges in Latvia for drone and counter-drone systems, and Ukraine is collaborating with Britain to develop advanced long-range maritime drones and underwater systems for operations beyond the Black Sea.
